Center: Center of Nuclear Physics Data, Russian Federal Nuclear Center (VNIIEF), Sarov, Russia
ref: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.239, p.172 (1975)
title: Elastic and inelastic scattering of identical heavy ions

x4freetext: Elastic and inelastic scattering of identical heavy
ions

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.239, p.172 (1975)
Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.211, p.600 (1973)

x4freetext: The EN and MP tandem Van de Graff
generators.

flag.hlp: Reference to related data compiled in another entry

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.211, p.600 (1973)

x4freetext: The detectors are mounted symmetrically to the
beam axis at forward angles and are operated in
coincidence. In the elastic scattering of identical
particles the angle between the direction of the two
scattered particles is 90. degr. For inelastic
scattering this angle is less than 90. degr.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.

reacs.QuantHlp: Differential c/s with respect to angle

x4freetext: The 12C beam was obtained from CO gas that decomposed
and ionized in the hydrogen discharge of a
duoplasmatron.
12C target: 20 ug/cm2 self-supporting natural carbon
foil.
The angular
distributions were normalized by the Rutherford cross
section of gold measured with the monitor detector in
order to obtain the differential cross sections. At
bombarding energies below 11 MeV, the measured cross
sections were adjusted to the Mott cross sections of
the 12C-12C scattering. A gold target of 25 ug/cm2
thickness was used.
The 12C particles scattered from gold were
detected with a silicon surface-barrier detector at
an angle of 35. degr. with respect to the beam.
Corrections due to the change of the solid angle of
the position-sensitive detector with scattering angle
were taken into account.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainties in the absolute cross
sections were estimated to be 10% for a scattering
angle of 90 degr. This estimate is based on the
accuracy of the normalization procedure between the
different runs and the uncertainties in setting
windows in the mass spectra.

x4code.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.211, p.600 (1973)

x4freetext: Fig.3 (left
side). Same data presented on fig.2 at
SD: Data were re-digitized. BIB updated.

x4freetext: The 12C beam was obtained from CO gas that decomposed
and ionized in the hydrogen discharge of a
duoplasmatron.
Residual nucleus.
Outgoing particle.
Inelastic scattering, where both the residual nucleus
and the outgoing particle are in the excited states,
is given.
12C target: 20 ug/cm2 self-supporting natural carbon
foil.
The angular
distributions were normalized by the Rutherford cross
section of gold measured with the monitor detector in
order to obtain the differential cross sections. At
bombarding energies below 11 MeV, the measured cross
sections were adjusted to the Mott cross sections of
the 12C-12C scattering. A gold target of 25 ug/cm2
thickness was used.
The 12C particles scattered from gold were
detected with a silicon surface-barrier detector at
an angle of 35. degr. with respect to the beam.
Corrections due to the change of the solid angle of
the position-sensitive detector with scattering angle
were taken into account.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainties in the absolute cross
sections were estimated to be 10% for a scattering
angle of 90 degr. This estimate is based on the
accuracy of the normalization procedure between the
different runs and the uncertainties in setting
windows in the mass spectra.

x4freetext: Fig.3 (left
side). Same data presented on fig.2 at

x4freetext: The 24Mg beam was prepared by evaporation of natural
magnesium from an oven into a Penning source which ran
on a hydrogen discharge. The quantity of negatively
charged 24Mg ions extracted from the source was of the
order of 20-30 nA.
24Mg target: 60 ug/cm2
isotopically enriched (99%) 24Mg evaporated onto
20 ug/cm2 carbon foil.
The angular
distributions were normalized by the Rutherford cross
section of gold measured with the monitor detector in
order to obtain the differential cross sections.
A gold target of 25 ug/cm2 thickness was used.
The 24Mg particles scattered from gold were
detected with a silicon surface-barrier detector at
an angle of 35. degr. with respect to the beam.
Corrections due to the change of the solid angle of
the position-sensitive detector with scattering angle
were taken into account.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: Absolute cross sections errors were
smaller than 20% in most of the measurements.
The errors in determining the scattering angles are
smaller than 1.degr.

x4code.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.239, p.172 (1975)

x4freetext: Fig.2
SD: BIB updated. EN-RSL added.

x4freetext: Angular distributions could be absolutely
reproduced within 10% uncertainty whereas variations
of less than 5% were found in the relative
intensities at different angles.

and ionized in the hydrogen discharge of a

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainties in the absolute cross
sections for forward and backward angles were might
increase to 20% due to the strong dependence of the
cross sections on angle.
From compiler: Compiler believes that it should be `mb`
instead of `mb/sr` as it is given on the fig.3 (right
side), because at the bottom of the fig.3 authors
wrote:`... integrated cross sections of the elastic
and inelastic 12C + 12C channels`.

x4code.shortRef: Jour: Nuclear Physics, Section A, Vol.211, p.600 (1973)

x4freetext: Fig.3 (right
side).
SD: Data were re-digitized. BIB updated.
SF6=DA -> IPA in REACTION code. EN-CM -> EN; second
col.ANG-CM-MIN ->ANG-CM-MAX. ANG=90. degr. deleted (not
relevant). COMMENT added. Units in the DATA col. were
changed (mb/sr -> mb, see COMMENT).
